title: Hottest Day Since '47

mood.label: Uneasy heat 🌥️
mood.summary: A heavy day under a record European heat dome, partly steadied by a Gulf thaw, cheaper oil and a few bright lights, but pinned just below neutral.

style.genre: post-rock / cinematic-strings — sampled grand piano laying slow open chords under a single sustained electric-guitar topline that swells and decays, with a bed of bowed strings, a marching brushed RhythmAce kit and a sine sub far below; G aeolian's natural minor sitting heavy but never sinking, a heat-haze that holds rather than breaks

sampleSources: Strudel default library (sampled piano, VCSL strings/guitar, RhythmAce, oscillator pads)
pattern: stack( note("<[g2,bb2,d3] [eb2,g2,bb2] [c3,eb3,g3] [d3,f3,bb3]>").s("piano").attack(0.01).release(1.6).gain(0.5).room(0.4).rsize(4).lpf(sine.range(900,2100).slow(16)), note("<g4 ~ bb4 ~> <~ d5 ~ c5>").s("gm_distortion_guitar").attack(0.18).sustain(0.6).release(1.2).gain(0.34).lpf(sine.range(1400,3200).slow(12)).room(0.5).rsize(5).delay(0.35).delaytime(0.5).delayfeedback(0.4).jux(rev), note("<g3 d4 bb3 eb4 c4 f4 d4 a3>").s("strings").attack(0.9).sustain(0.8).release(2.5).gain(0.22).lpf(1600).room(0.6).rsize(6), s("bd ~ ~ sd ~ ~ bd sd, ~ hh ~ hh ~ hh ~ hh").bank("RhythmAce").gain(0.46).room(0.18).hpf(180).swingBy(0.04,4), note("g1 ~ g1 d2 ~ eb1 ~ c2").s("sine").attack(0.02).release(0.5).lpf(420).gain(0.5) )

lines: Forty-four in Pissos, the highest since they kept the score,
the grid gave out in Finistère, dark windows by the shore.
They shut the Louvre at noon, no shade left to be found,
and somewhere far from all of it, the oil came drifting down.
Hottest day since 'forty-seven, and the air won't let us go,
but the Gulf kept on thawing, and the markets running slow.
Hold the line just under even, half-gold and half-aflame —
a heat that won't break, a day that won't name.
In Congo the count keeps climbing, in Darfur the lean months press,
but Messi found the net again, and the vultures answered yes.
A Viking loom unburied, a forever-chemical undone —
small lights against a furnace, holding on till the sun.
